title: lamium maculatum var. niveum
intro: Lamium maculatum var. niveum, also known as white-spotted deadnettle, is a perennial flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral activities. In herbal preparations, Lamium maculatum var. niveum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used in skincare products due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a popular ingredient in natural cosmetics.
